---
title: "Unique_Coordinates_Occurences"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-19"
---


```{r barnswallow_code}

library(tidyverse)

# ebird txt insert
file_path <- "~/Downloads/C&SBIO_185_PROJECT/REAL_DATA_C&S_BIO_185_PROJECT/barnswallow_smp_rel.txt"

# read
ebird_data_barnswallow <- read_tsv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)

# count unique coordinate pairs
barnswallow_unique_coordinates <- ebird_data_barnswallow %>%
  filter(!is.na(LATITUDE), !is.na(LONGITUDE)) %>%
  group_by(LATITUDE, LONGITUDE) %>%
  summarise(
    occurrence_count = n(),
    locality = first(LOCALITY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(occurrence_count), LATITUDE, LONGITUDE)

# show them
print(barnswallow_unique_coordinates)

# output
write_csv(barnswallow_unique_coordinates, "REAL_barn_swallow_unique_coordinate_pairs_with_counts.csv")


# San Diego city center coordinates
sd_lat <- 32.7157
sd_lon <- -117.1611

# Haversine distance function
haversine_km <- function(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) {
  R <- 6371 # Earth radius in km
  
  lat1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  lon1 <- lon1 * pi / 180
  lat2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  lon2 <- lon2 * pi / 180
  
  dlat <- lat2 - lat1
  dlon <- lon2 - lon1
  
  a <- sin(dlat / 2)^2 +
    cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon / 2)^2
  
  c <- 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1 - a))
  
  distance <- R * c
  return(distance)
}

# Add distance from San Diego to each coordinate pair
barnswallow_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <- haversine_km(
  sd_lat,
  sd_lon,
  barnswallow_unique_coordinates$LATITUDE,
  barnswallow_unique_coordinates$LONGITUDE
)

# Filter to only coordinates within 100 km
barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km <- barnswallow_unique_coordinates[
  barnswallow_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <= 100,
]

# Sort from closest to farthest
barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km <- barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km[
  order(barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km$distance_from_san_diego_km),
]

# View result
print(barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km,
  "REAL_barnswallow_coordinates_within_100km_of_san_diego.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r california_quail}
file_path <- "~/Downloads/C&SBIO_185_PROJECT/REAL_DATA_C&S_BIO_185_PROJECT/californiaquail_smp_rel.txt"

ebird_data_california_quail <- read_tsv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)


california_quail_unique_coordinates <- ebird_data_california_quail %>%
  filter(!is.na(LATITUDE), !is.na(LONGITUDE)) %>%
  group_by(LATITUDE, LONGITUDE) %>%
  summarise(
    occurrence_count = n(),
    locality = first(LOCALITY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(occurrence_count), LATITUDE, LONGITUDE)


print(california_quail_unique_coordinates)
write_csv(california_quail_unique_coordinates, "REAL_california_quail_unique_coordinate_pairs_with_counts.csv")

sd_lat <- 32.7157
sd_lon <- -117.1611

haversine_km <- function(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) {
  R <- 6371 
  
  lat1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  lon1 <- lon1 * pi / 180
  lat2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  lon2 <- lon2 * pi / 180
  
  dlat <- lat2 - lat1
  dlon <- lon2 - lon1
  
  a <- sin(dlat / 2)^2 +
    cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon / 2)^2
  
  c <- 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1 - a))
  
  distance <- R * c
  return(distance)
}

california_quail_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <- haversine_km(
  sd_lat,
  sd_lon,
  california_quail_unique_coordinates$LATITUDE,
  california_quail_unique_coordinates$LONGITUDE
)

# Filter to only coordinates within 100 km
california_quail_coordinates_within_100km <- california_quail_unique_coordinates[
  california_quail_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <= 100,
]

# Sort from closest to farthest
california_quail_coordinates_within_100km <- california_quail_coordinates_within_100km[
  order(california_quail_coordinates_within_100km$distance_from_san_diego_km),
]

# View result
print(california_quail_coordinates_within_100km)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  california_quail_coordinates_within_100km,
  "REAL_california_quail_coordinates_within_100km_of_san_diego.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

```

```{r house sparrow}
file_path <- "~/Downloads/C&SBIO_185_PROJECT/REAL_DATA_C&S_BIO_185_PROJECT/housesparrow_smp_rel.txt"

ebird_data_housesparrow <- read_tsv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)


housesparrow_unique_coordinates <- ebird_data_housesparrow %>%
  filter(!is.na(LATITUDE), !is.na(LONGITUDE)) %>%
  group_by(LATITUDE, LONGITUDE) %>%
  summarise(
    occurrence_count = n(),
    locality = first(LOCALITY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(occurrence_count), LATITUDE, LONGITUDE)


print(housesparrow_unique_coordinates)
write_csv(housesparrow_unique_coordinates, "REAL_housesparrow_unique_coordinate_pairs_with_counts.csv")

sd_lat <- 32.7157
sd_lon <- -117.1611

haversine_km <- function(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) {
  R <- 6371 
  
  lat1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  lon1 <- lon1 * pi / 180
  lat2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  lon2 <- lon2 * pi / 180
  
  dlat <- lat2 - lat1
  dlon <- lon2 - lon1
  
  a <- sin(dlat / 2)^2 +
    cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon / 2)^2
  
  c <- 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1 - a))
  
  distance <- R * c
  return(distance)
}

housesparrow_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <- haversine_km(
  sd_lat,
  sd_lon,
  housesparrow_unique_coordinates$LATITUDE,
  housesparrow_unique_coordinates$LONGITUDE
)

# Filter to only coordinates within 100 km
housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km <- housesparrow_unique_coordinates[
  housesparrow_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <= 100,
]

# Sort from closest to farthest
housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km <- housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km[
  order(housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km$distance_from_san_diego_km),
]

# View result
print(housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km,
  "REAL_housesparrow_coordinates_within_100km_of_san_diego.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r peregrine falcon}
file_path <- "~/Downloads/C&SBIO_185_PROJECT/REAL_DATA_C&S_BIO_185_PROJECT/peregrinefalcon_smp_rel.txt"

ebird_data_peregrinefalcon <- read_tsv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)


peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates <- ebird_data_peregrinefalcon %>%
  filter(!is.na(LATITUDE), !is.na(LONGITUDE)) %>%
  group_by(LATITUDE, LONGITUDE) %>%
  summarise(
    occurrence_count = n(),
    locality = first(LOCALITY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(occurrence_count), LATITUDE, LONGITUDE)


print(peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates)
write_csv(peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates, "REAL_peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinate_pairs_with_counts.csv")

sd_lat <- 32.7157
sd_lon <- -117.1611

haversine_km <- function(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) {
  R <- 6371 
  
  lat1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  lon1 <- lon1 * pi / 180
  lat2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  lon2 <- lon2 * pi / 180
  
  dlat <- lat2 - lat1
  dlon <- lon2 - lon1
  
  a <- sin(dlat / 2)^2 +
    cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon / 2)^2
  
  c <- 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1 - a))
  
  distance <- R * c
  return(distance)
}

peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <- haversine_km(
  sd_lat,
  sd_lon,
  peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates$LATITUDE,
  peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates$LONGITUDE
)

# Filter to only coordinates within 100 km
peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km <- peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates[
  peregrinefalcon_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <= 100,
]

# Sort from closest to farthest
peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km <- peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km[
  order(peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km$distance_from_san_diego_km),
]

# View result
print(peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km,
  "REAL_peregrinefalcon_coordinates_within_100km_of_san_diego.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r rock pigeon}
file_path <- "~/Downloads/C&SBIO_185_PROJECT/REAL_DATA_C&S_BIO_185_PROJECT/rockpigeon_smp_rel.txt"

ebird_data_rockpigeon <- read_tsv(file_path, show_col_types = FALSE)


rockpigeon_unique_coordinates <- ebird_data_rockpigeon %>%
  filter(!is.na(LATITUDE), !is.na(LONGITUDE)) %>%
  group_by(LATITUDE, LONGITUDE) %>%
  summarise(
    occurrence_count = n(),
    locality = first(LOCALITY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(occurrence_count), LATITUDE, LONGITUDE)


print(rockpigeon_unique_coordinates)
write_csv(rockpigeon_unique_coordinates, "REAL_rockpigeon_unique_coordinate_pairs_with_counts.csv")

sd_lat <- 32.7157
sd_lon <- -117.1611

haversine_km <- function(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) {
  R <- 6371 
  
  lat1 <- lat1 * pi / 180
  lon1 <- lon1 * pi / 180
  lat2 <- lat2 * pi / 180
  lon2 <- lon2 * pi / 180
  
  dlat <- lat2 - lat1
  dlon <- lon2 - lon1
  
  a <- sin(dlat / 2)^2 +
    cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon / 2)^2
  
  c <- 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1 - a))
  
  distance <- R * c
  return(distance)
}

rockpigeon_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <- haversine_km(
  sd_lat,
  sd_lon,
  rockpigeon_unique_coordinates$LATITUDE,
  rockpigeon_unique_coordinates$LONGITUDE
)

# Filter to only coordinates within 100 km
rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km <- rockpigeon_unique_coordinates[
  rockpigeon_unique_coordinates$distance_from_san_diego_km <= 100,
]

# Sort from closest to farthest
rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km <- rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km[
  order(rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km$distance_from_san_diego_km),
]

# View result
print(rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km)

# Save as CSV
write.csv(
  rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km,
  "REAL_rockpigeon_coordinates_within_100km_of_san_diego.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```
